expectations.text: Tells user to create a GPU session with colab new --gpu
expectations.evidence: Line 39: `colab new --gpu T4 --session cifar-run` preceded by the comment "# Create a T4 GPU session"

expectations.text: Tells user to upload script files with colab upload
expectations.evidence: Lines 42-44 show three colab upload commands: `colab upload ~/ml/train_cifar.py train_cifar.py`, `colab upload launch.py launch.py`, `colab upload check_progress.py check_progress.py`

expectations.text: Uses start_new_session or subprocess.Popen pattern for background execution
expectations.evidence: Line 58: "training continues running because `start_new_session=True` detaches it from the Colab kernel". Also line 5: "launch the training as a detached nohup-style subprocess"

expectations.text: Mentions PYTHONUNBUFFERED or python -u for log output
expectations.evidence: Line 23: "spawns your script as a detached subprocess with unbuffered stdout (`-u` flag + `PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1`)". Also line 139 for reinforcement.

expectations.text: Mentions installing torch, torchvision, wandb dependencies
expectations.evidence: Line 19: `DEPS = ["torch", "torchvision", "wandb"]`. Line 23: "This installs the three dependencies". Lines 57-58: "gives pip time to install torch, torchvision, and wandb"

expectations.text: Mentions using relative paths with colab exec -f
expectations.evidence: Line 136: "**`colab exec -f` requires relative paths.** Upload to `/content/train_cifar.py` but exec with `-f train_cifar.py`."
Line 7: "Session lifetime is ~2-4 hours on free tier". Line 109: "Free-tier Colab sessions auto-terminate after approximately 2-4 hours of total runtime."

expectations.text: Mentions colab download to retrieve checkpoints
expectations.evidence: Lines 86-90: `colab download checkpoints/` and individual file examples. Line 113: "The `colab download` command works while the session is still alive."

expectations.text: Shows how to check on running training (pgrep, log tail, or check_progress script)
expectations.evidence: Lines 64-78: shows `colab exec -f check_progress.py` which "shows: whether the process is alive, the last 15 log lines, and any checkpoint files", plus a monitoring loop with `while true; do ... colab exec -f check_progress.py ... sleep 300; done`
